system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoCentralFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.2;

deltaT          0.0001;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.02;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable yes;

adjustTimeStep  no;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

fluxScheme      Kurganov;

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(tauMC)      Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
    reconstruct(rho) vanLeer;
    reconstruct(U)  vanLeerV;
    reconstruct(T)  vanLeer;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "(rho|rhoU|rhoE)"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        nSweeps         2;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    e
    {
        $U;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  laminar;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
    energy          sensibleInternalEnergy;
}

mixture
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight   11640.3;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          2.5;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          0;
        Pr          1;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 1 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform (5 0 0);

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform (5 0 0);
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            inletOutlet;
        inletValue      uniform (5 0 0);
        value           uniform (5 0 0);
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    top
    {
        type            inletOutlet;
        inletValue      uniform (5 0 0);
        value           uniform (5 0 0);
    }

    wedge
    {
        type            slip;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='T', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 0 0 1 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    top
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    wedge
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [1 -1 -2 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            waveTransmissive;
        field           p;
        psi             thermo:psi;
        gamma           1.4;
        fieldInf        1;
        lInf            1;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    top
    {
        type            waveTransmissive;
        field           p;
        psi             thermo:psi;
        gamma           1.4;
        fieldInf        1;
        lInf            1;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    wedge
    {
        type            slip;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      blockMeshDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - lower region below wedge
    (-0.15242  0        -0.005)  // 0
    ( 0          0        -0.005)  // 1
    ( 0          0.09144  -0.005)  // 2
    (-0.15242  0.09144  -0.005)  // 3

    ( 0          0         0.005)  // 4
    ( 0          0.09144   0.005)  // 5
    (-0.15242  0.09144   0.005)  // 6
    (-0.15242  0         0.005)  // 7

    // Block 1 - upper region above wedge
    ( 0          0.09144  -0.005)  // 8
    ( 0.3048     0.09144  -0.005)  // 9
    ( 0.3048     0.1524   -0.005)  // 10
    ( 0          0.1524   -0.005)  // 11

    ( 0          0.09144   0.005)  // 12
    ( 0.3048     0.09144   0.005)  // 13
    ( 0.3048     0.1524    0.005)  // 14
    ( 0          0.1524    0.005)  // 15
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (30 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15) (60 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (9 10 14 13)
        );
    }

    top
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (8 12 13 9)
            (9 13 14 10)
        );
    }

    bottom
    {
        type symmetryPlane;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
        );
    }

    wedge
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (3 7 6 2)
        );
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
            (4 5 6 7)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //')]</foamfiles>

<reviewer_analysis>## Error Analysis

### 1. `log.blockMesh` — Internal face misidentification

**Error:**  
`Trying to specify a boundary face 4(9 13 14 10) on the face on cell 1 which is either an internal face or already belongs to some other patch.`

This means face `(9 13 14 10)` is being treated as a boundary face, but it's actually an **internal face** shared between the two blocks. Looking at the vertex coordinates more carefully:

Block 0 (lower): vertices 0(-0.15242,0,-0.005), 1(0,0,-0.005), 2(0,0.09144,-0.005), 3(-0.15242,0.09144,-0.005)  
Block 1 (upper): vertices 8(0,0.09144,-0.005), 9(0.3048,0.09144,-0.005), 10(0.3048,0.1524,-0.005), 11(0,0.1524,-0.005)

The face `(9 13 14 10)` connects vertices 9→13→14→10. Vertex 13 is **(0.3048, 0.1524, 0.005)** — the top-right corner of block 1. But vertex 10 is **(0.3048, 0.1524, -0.005)** — the bottom-right corner of block 1. So this face is vertical (along +z direction), not horizontal. It's actually the **right side face** of block 1, which should indeed be a boundary face (outlet).

However, the real problem is that **both blocks share the face defined by vertices (8,9,10,11)** at y = 0.09144. This is the interface between the two blocks and should be an **internal face**, not listed as a boundary patch.

But looking at the current `blockMeshDict`:

- `top`: contains `(8 12 13 9)` and `(9 13 14 10)` — these are the **horizontal top faces** of each block (correct).
- `frontAndBack`: contains `(0 3 2 1)` and `(4 5 6 7)` — these are the **horizontal bottom faces** of each block (incorrect — they should be internal/top/bottom depending on orientation).

Wait — let me recheck the vertex layout. Actually, looking more carefully:

Vertices 0,1,2,3 are at z=-0.005 (bottom layer)  
Vertices 4,5,6,7 are at z=+0.005 (top layer)  
Vertices 8,9,10,11 are at z=-0.005 (bottom layer)  
Vertices 12,13,14,15 are at z=+0.005 (top layer)

So the **shared internal face** between the two blocks is actually the face connecting vertices (3,2,10,7)? No — wait, block 0 goes from x=-0.15242 to 0, while block 1 goes from x=0 to 0.3048. They don't overlap in x-space! There's a gap at x=0. That means there's no shared face — the blocks are adjacent but not overlapping. Each block occupies its own x-range.

Actually, looking again: Block 0 has vertices with x ∈ [-0.15242, 0], Block 1 has vertices with x ∈ [0, 0.3048]. At x=0, they touch but don't share faces. The face at x=0 would involve vertices like (1,2,5,4) for block 0 and (8,9,12,13) for block 1 — but neither of these are listed as boundary faces.

The issue is that **all listed boundary faces are valid except possibly `frontAndBack`**. Let me check what face index 4 refers to. In blockMesh, face indexing follows the order they're encountered during mesh construction. The error says face 4 of patch "top" is `(9 13 14 10)`. Looking at the `top` patch definition, it has two entries: `(8 12 13 9)` and `(9 13 14 10)`. So face index 4 is the second entry `(9 13 14 10)`.

This face exists geometrically — it connects vertices 9(0.3048,0.09144,-0.005), 13(0.3048,0.1524,0.005), 14(0.3048,0.1524,0.005), 10(0.3048,0.1524,-0.005). Wait — vertices 13 and 14 are identical! That's impossible. Vertex 13 = (0.3048, 0.1524, 0.005), vertex 14 = (0.3048, 0.1524, 0.005) — they're the same point!

That's the bug! **Vertex 14 is duplicated.** Looking at the vertex list:

```
( 0.3048     0.1524   -0.005)  // 10
( 0          0.1524    0.005)  // 11
( 0.3048     0.1524    0.005)  // 14
```

No — vertex 14 is correct. The issue is that I misread. Vertex 10 is at z=-0.005, vertex 14 is at z=+0.005. Both have y=0.1524, x=0.3048. So face `(9 13 14 10)` is a vertical face at x=0.3048 going from y=0.09144,z=-0.005 up to y=0.1524,z=+0.005 — this is the right-side face of block 1, which should be a boundary face (part of the outlet patch).

But the error says this face is already assigned to another patch. Checking the `frontAndBack` patch: it includes `(0 3 2 1)` and `(4 5 6 7)`. Neither of these involves vertices 9,13,14,10. However, looking back at Attempt 1 history, the previous error was about face `(11 15 14 10)` belonging to `frontAndBack`. Now it's `(9 13 14 10)` — so somehow the face assignment changed.

Let me look at the current `blockMeshDict` again. The `frontAndBack` patch has:

```
(0 3 2 1)
(4 5 6 7)
```

Neither of these matches `(9 13 14 10)`. So why is face 4 of the `top` patch conflicting?

Ah — I think the issue is that **face `(9 13 14 10)` is actually the same geometric face as one of the faces in `frontAndBack`**, just indexed differently due to how blockMesh constructs the face list internally. When multiple blocks share a common edge or vertex arrangement, the face ordering can cause conflicts.

The fundamental problem is that **the `frontAndBack` patch is wrong**. With this vertex layout, the faces `(0 3 2 1)` and `(4 5 6 7)` are the **bottom faces** of the blocks (at z=-0.005), not front/back faces. And the faces at x=±0.15242 and x=0.3048 need to be properly assigned.

## Recommended Fixes

### For `system/blockMeshDict`:

1. **Remove the `frontAndBack` patch entirely** — with this vertex layout and block decomposition, there are no true front/back faces. The only external faces are:
   - Left face at x=-0.15242 (wedge side)
   - Right face at x=0.3048 (outlet)
   - Bottom face at z=-0.005 (but this is split between blocks — needs careful handling)
   - Top face at z=+0.005 (split between blocks)

2. **Reassign the faces properly**:
   - `inlet`: keep `(0 4 7 3)` — this is the left vertical face at x=-0.15242 (wedge side)
   - `outlet`: keep `(9 10 14 13)` — this is the right vertical face at x=0.3048
   - `top`: keep the two horizontal top faces `(8 12 13 9)` and `(9 13 14 10)`
   - `bottom`: keep `(0 1 5 4)` — this is the bottom horizontal face at z=-0.005, spanning both blocks (this is correct)
   - **Remove `frontAndBack`** — there are no front/back faces in this configuration

3. **Ensure no duplicate or conflicting face definitions**

Alternatively, if `frontAndBack` is intended to represent the faces at x=0 (where the two blocks meet), then the face should be something like `(1 2 11 10)` and `(4 5 12 13)` — but since the blocks don't actually share a face (they're adjacent at x=0 with no overlapping face), this wouldn't work either.

The cleanest fix is to remove `frontAndBack` and ensure all boundary faces are unique and correctly identified.

### For `0/U`, `0/T`, `0/p`

The boundary conditions appear consistent with the wedge15Ma5 reference case and the user requirements, so no changes needed there.

### Summary

| File | Issue | Fix |
|------|-------|-----|
| `system/blockMeshDict` | Invalid `frontAndBack` patch with face indices that may conflict with other patches | **Remove the `frontAndBack` patch entirely** since there are no true front/back faces in this 3D wedge geometry. All six boundaries (inlet, outlet, top, bottom, wedge, plus the missing side at x=0.3048 which is already `outlet`) should be sufficient. Verify all listed face indices correspond to actual distinct faces. |</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Remove the entire frontAndBack patch entry from the boundary section since there are no true front/back faces in this 3D wedge geometry; Keep inlet as the left vertical face at x=-0.15242 using face (0 4 7 3); Keep outlet as the right vertical face at x=0.3048 using face (9 10 14 13); Keep top as the two horizontal top faces using (8 12 13 9) and (9 13 14 10); Keep bottom as the bottom horizontal face at z=-0.005 using face (0 1 5 4) which spans across both blocks'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Perform a 2D supersonic flow simulation over a wedge. Create a 3D geometry spanning from -0.15242 m to 0.3048 m in the x-direction, 0 m to 0.1524 m in the y-direction, and -0.005 m to 0.005 m in the z-direction. The simulation starts at 0.0 seconds and runs until 0.2 seconds with a time step (deltaT) of 0.0001 seconds. The results are written every 0.02 seconds. The inlet velocity is 5 m/s, inlet temperature is 1 K and inlet pressure is 1 Pa. The wedge boundary uses slip boundary condition for velocity. Use normalized thermodynamic properties where molWeight is set to 11640.3, Cp is 2.5, Pr is 1.0, and dynamic viscosity mu is 0. This corresponds to a gas constant R of 0.7143 and a specific heat ratio of 1.4. Use rhoCentralFoam solver.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
